book_claims.claim: Recommended for those over 65 losing muscle mass, consumed in high-longevity areas
book_claims.mechanism: Goat's milk proteins and probiotics support gut health; easier digestion than cow's milk

supplementary_research.finding: Goat milk fermented products show higher bioavailability of calcium and phosphorus than cow milk, with probiotic strains improving gut microbiota diversity in older adults.
Goat milk proteins form softer curds in the stomach, improving digestibility and reducing gastrointestinal inflammation compared to cow milk.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Higher bioavailability than cow milk due to smaller fat globules and different casein structure
Survive gastric transit better in goat milk matrix; colonize colon effectively
Rapidly absorbed, support gut epithelial integrity and have antimicrobial properties

synergies.description: Omega-3 fats in walnuts complement the anti-inflammatory capric acid in goat yogurt, supporting gut-brain axis health
Polyphenols in olive oil enhance absorption of fat-soluble vitamins (A, D) in goat yogurt
Anthocyanins in blueberries and probiotics in yogurt together enhance gut microbiota diversity and reduce oxidative stress
